inputs.label: Current Interest Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: The prevailing market interest rate for the reference period.

businessContext_i18n.en: The prevailing market interest rate for the reference period.

inputs.label: Expected Rate Change (bps)
inputs.businessContext: Anticipated shift in interest rate in basis points (1 bps = 0.01%).

businessContext_i18n.en: Anticipated shift in interest rate in basis points (1 bps = 0.01%).

inputs.label: Portfolio Value (USD)
inputs.businessContext: Total notional value of the fixed-income portfolio.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total notional value of the fixed-income portfolio.

inputs.label: Modified Duration (years)
inputs.businessContext: Sensitivity of bond price to yield changes, adjusted for yield.

businessContext_i18n.en: Sensitivity of bond price to yield changes, adjusted for yield.

inputs.label: Convexity
inputs.businessContext: Second-order measure of curvature in price-yield relationship.

businessContext_i18n.en: Second-order measure of curvature in price-yield relationship.

inputs.label: Hedge Ratio (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of portfolio hedged using derivatives or swaps.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of portfolio hedged using derivatives or swaps.

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Statistical confidence for VaR and tail risk estimation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Statistical confidence for VaR and tail risk estimation.

inputs.label: Volatility Regime
inputs.businessContext: Market volatility environment for stress testing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Market volatility environment for stress testing.

rules.message: Convexity is unusually low relative to duration. Verify inputs.
Hedge ratio must be between 0% and 100%.
Extreme volatility regime typically requires rate change > 100 bps.

thresholds.message: Projected loss exceeds 5% of portfolio value. Immediate review recommended.
Low convexity amplifies risk under large rate shifts.

formulas.name: Duration-based price change
formulas.description: First-order approximation of price change using modified duration.

formulas.name: Convexity adjustment
formulas.description: Second-order convexity correction for price change.

formulas.name: Total price change (unhedged)
formulas.description: Combined duration and convexity effect on portfolio value.

formulas.name: Hedged price change
formulas.description: Net price change after applying hedge ratio.

formulas.name: Value at Risk (VaR)
formulas.description: Statistical loss estimate at given confidence level under normal volatility.

formulas.name: Data confidence adjusted loss
formulas.description: Adjusts the hedged loss by a confidence factor based on input quality and volatility regime.

formulas.name: Net Interest Rate Risk Exposure
formulas.description: Primary result: expected financial impact after hedging and confidence adjustment.

primary.label: Net Interest Rate Risk Exposure
primary.description: Expected change in portfolio value after hedging and confidence adjustment.

hedged_impact.label: Hedged Impact
hedged_impact.description: Net impact after applying hedge ratio.

value_at_risk.label: Value at Risk (VaR)
value_at_risk.description: Statistical loss at selected confidence level.

hiddenLossDrivers.name: Parallel Shift Assumption
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Assumes all maturities shift equally; non-parallel shifts may cause additional basis risk.

hiddenLossDrivers.name: Liquidity Spread Compression
hiddenLossDrivers.description: In stressed markets, liquidity premiums may erode, amplifying losses beyond duration/convexity estimates.

hiddenLossDrivers.name: Counterparty Credit Risk on Hedges
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Hedge effectiveness may degrade if counterparty defaults or collateral calls are missed.

hiddenLossDrivers.name: Embedded Options (Call/Put)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Bonds with embedded options have negative convexity at certain yield levels, not captured by standard convexity.

suggestedActions.description: Increase hedge ratio to 80% if volatility regime is high or extreme.
Diversify into floating-rate notes to reduce duration exposure.
Implement interest rate swaps to lock in current rates for 6-12 months.
Conduct scenario analysis for non-parallel yield curve shifts (e.g., steepening/flattening).
Review counterparty credit limits and collateral agreements for hedge positions.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Loss
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Loss figure adjusted for volatility regime and input reliability.
